profile.name: Rober
profile.role_in_household: Primary client; historically the higher earner and the one who tracks investments and planning details, even while currently unemployed.
profile.financial_literacy_level: High — comfortable with investment concepts, account types, and tax implications; tends to ask for assumptions and wants to see the math behind recommendations.
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Growth-oriented and willing to tolerate volatility if the plan is coherent; prefers taking calculated risk rather than sitting in cash, but gets more cautious when timelines (retirement in the next decade) are discussed.
profile.communication_style: Direct and detail-focused; will correct small inaccuracies and quote specific figures when he remembers them. Uses occasional hedges like "roughly" or "if I’m remembering right" when unsure, and may pause to think before answering.
profile.decision_making_style: Research-and-verify: wants options, tradeoffs, and a recommendation; will decide after confirming details and aligning with goals.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Cautiously open — willing to engage, but expects competence and transparency; trust increases quickly if the advisor demonstrates precision and follow-through.
internal_biases: overconfidence in ability to time or optimize decisions
recency bias (recent market moves influence comfort level)
anchoring to prior income level when thinking about affordability
hidden_concerns: how the current unemployment period affects retirement timing and identity
fear of making an irreversible mistake with taxable investments or alternative holdings
worry about being the sole driver of financial decisions

profile.name: Elizabeth
profile.role_in_household: Spouse/partner; steady earner and day-to-day budget voice, more focused on cash flow, health, and near-term security than portfolio construction.
profile.financial_literacy_level: Moderate — understands basics (spending, saving, retirement accounts) but mixes up technical terms (gross vs. net, premium vs. benefit, balance vs. payment) and prefers plain-language explanations.
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: More cautious in practice: supports growth goals but becomes risk-averse when conversations turn to market downturns or health-related uncertainty; prefers buffers and predictable outcomes.
profile.communication_style: Warm, conversational, and story-based; speaks in round numbers and approximations ("about," "give or take"). Will ask clarifying questions and sometimes defer to Robert on investment specifics, but will interrupt if something feels too risky or unclear.
emotional_tendencies: stress when discussions feel overly technical
relief when there’s a clear safety net
profile.decision_making_style: Consensus-seeking: wants to feel heard and to understand the practical impact; prefers a short list of choices and a clear recommendation.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Neutral-to-guarded at first — trust builds through empathy, patience, and avoiding jargon; responds well to summaries and checklists.
internal_biases: loss aversion (downside feels more salient than upside)
status quo bias (prefers not to change accounts unless the benefit is obvious)
hidden_concerns: health and longevity uncertainty affecting retirement readiness
fear that investment risk could threaten lifestyle stability
concern about being left out of decisions due to technical complexity
